concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Measures the amount of computer memory that an algorithm requires to run.

concepts.name: Auxiliary Space
concepts.short_definition: The temporary space used by an algorithm, excluding the input space.

concepts.name: Input Space
concepts.short_definition: The space required to store the input data for an algorithm.

concepts.name: Constant Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that uses a fixed amount of memory, regardless of input size.

concepts.name: Linear Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose memory usage grows linearly with the size of the input.

concepts.name: Logarithmic Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose memory usage grows logarithmically with the size of the input.

concepts.name: Quadratic Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose memory usage grows quadratically with the size of the input.

concepts.name: Big O Notation
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical notation used to describe the limiting behavior of a function, often used for complexity analysis.

concepts.name: Recursion Space
concepts.short_definition: The memory used by the call stack during recursive function calls.

concepts.name: Data Structures Space
concepts.short_definition: The memory required to store and manage data using various data structures.
